decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Brightglass Gearhulk, supported by mana ramp from cards like Bloom Tender and Nurturing Pixie. The strategy also includes disruption with cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Sewer-veillance Cam to control the game tempo. Additionally, Boomerang Basics and Nature's Rhythm provide additional removal and tempo plays to keep the pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Seam Rip and Cryogen Relic also offer utility and protection for key creatures.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with efficient and aggressive creatures, utilizing cards like Leatherhead, Swamp Stalker, Pawpatch Recruit, and Badgermole Cub. The deck also includes spells like Practiced Offense and Seam Rip to maintain pressure and disrupt your opponent's game plan. Multiversal Passage and Temple Garden provide mana fixing and flexibility for casting your spells, while cards like Surrak, Elusive Hunter and Brightglass Gearhulk serve as powerful finishers. Overall, this deck focuses on deploying a fast and powerful board presence to quickly close out games.

The main concept of this deck is to control the opponent's board and hand through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. By using cards like Spell Pierce, Burst Lightning, and Prismari Charm, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while developing its own board presence with creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Hearth Elemental. The inclusion of cards like Impractical Joke and Winternight Stories adds an element of randomness and surprise to keep the opponent off balance. The overall strategy is to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through careful resource management.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and relentless assault using efficient creatures and pump spells. The deck aims to establish an early board presence with cards like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub, then use cards like Practiced Offense and Seam Rip to pump up the creatures and push through damage. Multiversal Passage and Hushwood Verge can help fix the mana base while cards like Jennifer Walters and Surrak, Elusive Hunter provide additional support and utility. The goal is to close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, counter spells, and card draw. It utilizes a mix of lands to ensure mana fixing and consistency. By consistently removing threats, countering spells, and drawing cards, the deck aims to outlast opponents and eventually win through incremental advantage. The deck also includes some powerful finishers like North Wind Avatar and Jeskai Revelation to seal the deal in the late game. Overall, the strategy revolves around controlling the game until the player can establish a dominant position and close out the game.

The main concept of the UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with quick, aggressive plays using low-cost spells and creatures. The deck focuses on tempo and disruption, utilizing cards like Boomerang Basics and Burst Lightning to control the board while creatures like Slickshot Show-Off and Eddymurk Crab deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes counter spells like Spell Pierce for protection and card draw spells like Opt to maintain card advantage. Multiversal Passage and Riverpyre Verge provide mana fixing, allowing for consistent plays throughout the game.

This deck focuses on achieving early aggression and tempo by utilizing low cost spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It relies on a combination of direct damage spells like Burst Lightning and Boomerang Basics, as well as creatures like Slickshot Show-Off and Colorstorm Stallion to apply pressure. The deck also includes card draw and bounce spells to maintain card advantage and control the board, while also providing some protection with cards like Spell Pierce. The key strategy is to outpace the opponent and deal damage quickly before they can stabilize.

This aggro Selesnya deck focuses on flooding the board with creatures and using combat tricks to overwhelm the opponent quickly. With efficient early game creatures like Llanowar Elves and Pawpatch Recruit, followed by powerful threats like Brightglass Gearhulk and Surrak, Elusive Hunter, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and quickly close out games. The inclusion of cards like Practiced Offense and Seam Rip provide additional ways to buff the board and push through damage, while the mix of lands and mana ramp ensures a consistent flow of threats throughout the game.

This deck focuses on using a variety of control spells and card draw to outmaneuver the opponent and eventually overpower them with powerful spells and creatures. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to maintain control of the board while steadily building towards a game-winning scenario. Key cards such as Jeskai Revelation and Gran-Gran provide card advantage and tempo swings, while Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson offer removal options to deal with threats. Tablet of Discovery and Riverpyre Verge help with consistency and mana fixing to ensure smooth gameplay.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize a combination of cards to reanimate powerful creatures and create a game-winning advantage. By using cards like Winternight Stories, Requiting Hex, and Doomsday Excruciator to fill the graveyard with creatures, and then using cards like Bitter Triumph and Superior Spider-Man to bring them back onto the battlefield, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. The deck also includes disruption and protection spells like Duress and Deadly Cover-Up to control the game and ensure the combo can be executed smoothly.

The main concept of this deck is centered around utilizing control elements to manipulate the game state and maintain card advantage, while also using Izzet-themed cards to deal damage and further control the board. By using cards like Combustion Technique, Firebending Lesson, and Jeskai Revelation, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and eventually win through a combination of damage and control. Additionally, cards like Stock Up and Accumulate Wisdom help to ensure that the player has access to the resources they need to maintain their strategy throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively play low-cost spells and creatures with the goal of overwhelming the opponent quickly. The deck focuses on tempo and efficiency, utilizing bounce spells and cheap creatures to control the board while dealing damage. By maintaining a high pace of play and consistently applying pressure, the deck aims to put the opponent on the back foot and secure a swift victory before the opponent can stabilize. The inclusion of various instant speed spells and card draw options also allow for adaptability and flexibility in responding to the opponent's actions.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of cheap, efficient spells and creatures. The deck aims to maintain board control and apply constant pressure through aggressive plays, utilizing cards like Boomerang Basics, Burst Lightning, and Stormchaser's Talent. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Impractical Joke and Spell Pierce to disrupt the opponent's game plan. By efficiently utilizing resources and applying consistent pressure, this UR Aggro deck aims to secure a quick victory against opponents in a 1 vs 1 Standard format.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of instant and sorcery spells to control the game, drawing cards and managing resources efficiently. By utilizing cards like Jeskai Revelation, Stock Up, and Accumulate Wisdom, the player can maintain card advantage and out-value their opponent. Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson provide removal and board control, while Iroh's Demonstration and Abandon Attachments offer additional utility. Tablet of Discovery and Great Hall of the Biblioplex help with card selection and mana fixing, ultimately allowing the player to outlast and outmaneuver their opponent.

The main strategy of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and aggressive playstyle, focusing on playing creatures and using combat tricks to deal damage efficiently. The deck includes cards that provide mana acceleration, creature buffs, and card advantage, allowing the player to maintain pressure on the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of a mix of low-cost and higher-cost creatures ensures that the player has options for different stages of the game. The goal is to build a board presence early and continue to push for damage until achieving victory.

The main concept of this Jeskai Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while drawing cards and gaining life to outvalue them. The deck utilizes a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw to control the board and win the long game. The key strategy is to efficiently answer threats, gain card advantage, and ultimately overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and card advantage. The deck also includes a few lands that provide additional utility to support its control strategy.
